perturbation.original_text: This Agreement shall automatically be renewed for successive one (1) year increments unless either party request in writing, at least ninety (90) days prior to the anniversary date, that this Agreement not to be renewed.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement shall automatically be renewed for successive one (1) year increments regardless of whether either party requests in writing, at least ninety (90) days prior to the anniversary date, that this Agreement not to be renewed. However, after each auto renewal this agreement shall be renewed at the discretion of the Contractor.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states the agreement auto-renews unless a party requests non-renewal in writing. The changed text creates an in-text contradiction by first stating the agreement auto-renews regardless of any written request, making the written notice clause meaningless and then stating that it can be not renewed at the discretion of the Contractor. This creates uncertainty about the actual renewal process because it contradicts the auto-renewal clause, giving Contractor unilateral rights on contract renewal, by adding that after the auto renewal the agreement will be at the Contractor's discretion to renew, thus making the terms contradictory and therefore unenforceable due to ambiguity.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2. Term This Agreement shall commence on the Effective Date and shall continue for an initial term of year as of the Effective Date. This Agreement shall automatically be renewed for successive one (1) year increments regardless of whether either party requests in writing, at least ninety (90) days prior to the anniversary date, that this Agreement not to be renewed. However, after each auto renewal this agreement shall be renewed at the discretion of the Contractor.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a contradiction to the auto-renewal clause. Initially, the agreement auto-renews unless a party requests otherwise. The changed text then asserts auto-renewal regardless of any such request, making the written notice clause meaningless. Furthermore, it adds that renewal is at the Contractor's discretion after auto-renewal, directly conflicting with the initial auto-renewal provision. This creates ambiguity regarding the renewal process, giving the Contractor unilateral control. This contradicts the contradicted text, which attempts to establish a clear auto-renewal process, resulting in an in-text contradiction as defined under structural flaws, since the contradicted clause has conflicting implications.

perturbation.original_text: Contractor shall permit Customer to audit its quality procedures, upon three (3) business day advance written notice to Contractor and shall provide all assistance which is reasonably necessary for Customer to evaluate the quality of the Products.
perturbation.changed_text: Contractor shall permit Customer to audit its quality procedures, upon three (3) business day advance written notice to Contractor. However, Contractor is not obligated to provide all assistance which is reasonably necessary for Customer to evaluate the quality of the Products. Contractor will determine the extent of the assistance provided.
perturbation.explanation: The original text grants the Customer the right to an audit of the quality procedures, with the Contractor providing necessary assistance. The modified text creates a contradiction by stating the Contractor is not obligated to provide all necessary assistance, making the audit right less effective and unclear about how helpful the audit is supposed to be due to the uncertainty about assistance. This clause creates uncertainty about the extent of the Customer's auditing rights and the obligations of the Contractor during the audit.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 9.1 Contractor shall permit Customer to audit its quality procedures, upon three (3) business day advance written notice to Contractor. However, Contractor is not obligated to provide all assistance which is reasonably necessary for Customer to evaluate the quality of the Products. Contractor will determine the extent of the assistance provided.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts the original intent and functionality of the audit clause as it appears in the contradicted text. The original text obligates the contractor to provide reasonable assistance during the audit. The changed text removes this obligation, allowing the contractor to limit assistance, which undermines the customer's right to a meaningful audit, thereby creating an in-text contradiction. This aligns with the definition of 'Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction' because the clause regarding assistance is placed in the same location with conflicting implications.
